newsSinceJun6: June 9 8-K: APi closed the Onyx-Fire Protection Services acquisition (Canadian inspection-first fire/life-safety provider, ~$190M annual revenue) on June 8 and raised FY2026 guidance to $8,575-8,775M net revenue (from $8.4-8.6B) and $1,165-1,225M adjusted EBITDA (from $1,140-1,200M); Q2 guidance reaffirmed ex-Onyx partial month. Also debuted on the 2026 Fortune 500 at #486 (June 8, cosmetic). Stock +2.1% today on the close/guidance bump - fundamental but largely a mechanical M&A roll-in, not organic upside. Data center end market still ~10-11% of 2026 revenue per the June 2 Q1 call.

sources.label: StockTitan - APG news feed (Onyx-Fire close, raised 2026 guidance, Fortune 500 debut)
SEC 8-K exhibit 99.1, filed June 9, 2026 (Onyx-Fire close and guidance update)
Motley Fool - APi Group Q1 2026 earnings transcript (data center ~10-11% of 2026 revenue)

verdicts.rankRationale: The Onyx-Fire close and guidance raise reinforce the inspection-led recurring-revenue moat at the heart of the latent-node thesis, but it is a ~2% bolt-on with a mechanical M&A guidance roll-in, not new data-center-specific evidence; with the stock up only 2.1% and not crowded, the latent setup is unchanged versus yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: Mandatory fire/life-safety inspection is a tollbooth on every high-density data center, and the Onyx-Fire deal plus raised FY26 guidance ($8.6-8.8B revenue, $1.17-1.23B EBITDA) show the recurring-services compounder executing while still absent from AI supply-chain screens.
verdicts.neutral: Real but small news: $190M of acquired revenue on an $18.7B-cap company, with the guidance raise roughly equal to the acquisition's contribution; data center exposure stays ~10-11% of revenue, so the AI-node intensity of the story did not change.
verdicts.bear: APi is a diversified services roll-up where AI/data center is a tenth of revenue; integration-heavy M&A cadence and modest organic leverage to AI capex make it a diluted way to play the bottleneck, capping how high it should rank on a latent-AI-nodes list.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Data-center service contract wins and inspection/monitoring revenue mix disclosure at Q2 results; further accretive fire-safety bolt-ons.
verdicts.primaryRisk: AI capex exposure stays diluted (~10% of revenue) while M&A integration and leverage absorb management attention, leaving the name a GDP-plus services compounder rather than an AI bottleneck.
